label: COMPLETE (baseline)
practice_areas: Conveyancing, Family Law, Wills & Probate, Commercial Property, Employment Law

interpretation.summary: The SRA now requires all law firms to use only the UK Sanctions List (maintained by OFSI) for sanctions screening. Firms must review and update their current screening processes to ensure compliance with this single authoritative source, and the SRA may take enforcement action if arrangements are inadequate.
interpretation.applicability_reasoning: This notice applies to Henderson & Clarke Solicitors because they handle conveyancing, commercial property, and wills & probate work, all of which involve client money and financial transactions. The notice explicitly states that firms handling property work, financial transactions, or client money are particularly at risk of enforcement action.
action_items: Review current sanctions screening processes to verify they reference only the UK Sanctions List maintained by OFSI as the authoritative source
If using third-party screening tools for conveyancing, commercial property, or probate matters, verify these tools are configured to use the UK Sanctions List
Update internal compliance procedures and policies to mandate use of the UK Sanctions List for all client screening
Train relevant staff (particularly those handling conveyancing, commercial property, and probate matters) on the requirement to use only the UK Sanctions List
Document the review and any changes made to screening arrangements to demonstrate compliance if subject to SRA enforcement review
interpretation.source_citation: SRA, 'Sanctions enforcement update — firms must use UK Sanctions List as sole authoritative source', published February 2026. Available at: https://www.sra.org.uk/news/news/sra-update-147-sanctions-enforcement/

interpretation.summary: The SRA now requires all law firms to use only the UK Sanctions List maintained by OFSI for sanctions screening, and to verify that any third-party screening tools also reference this single authoritative source. Firms with inadequate screening processes may face enforcement action, particularly those handling financial transactions, property work, or client money.
interpretation.applicability_reasoning: The notice creates a mandatory obligation for all law firms, but without knowing Henderson & Clarke's practice areas, I cannot determine whether they handle the high-risk work types mentioned (financial transactions, property work, or client money). If they do handle any of these areas, this notice clearly applies.
action_items: Confirm whether your firm handles financial transactions, property work, or client money
If you handle any of these areas, review your current sanctions screening processes to ensure they use only the UK Sanctions List maintained by OFSI as the authoritative source
If you use third-party screening tools, verify that they reference the UK Sanctions List and contact providers if necessary to confirm compliance
Document your review and any changes made to screening arrangements to demonstrate compliance if the SRA conducts an inspection
interpretation.source_citation: SRA, 'Sanctions enforcement update — firms must use UK Sanctions List as sole authoritative source', published February 2026. Available at: https://www.sra.org.uk/news/news/sra-update-147-sanctions-enforcement/

Firms must review and update their screening processes, and the SRA may take enforcement action against firms with inadequate arrangements, particularly those handling financial transactions, property work, or client money.

Document your sanctions screening arrangements in case of SRA enforcement review
